title: Reserved-only tag payload can create an empty label

evidence.quote: it("rejects a payload with neither name nor tags", async () => {
it("strips reserved server-provenance tags (arkham, minipay) from user input", async () => {

reasoning: The non-empty validation runs before reserved server-provenance tags are stripped. A request like { address, name: "", tags: ["arkham", "minipay"] } passes the parsedTags.length check, then both tags are removed, and upsertEntry is called with an empty name and empty tag list. That contradicts the route's stated invariant and can persist a label that the normal validator says should be rejected.
reproduction: POST/PUT an authenticated request to /api/address-labels with a valid address, empty name, and only reserved tags such as ["arkham"]. The handler reaches upsertEntry with { name: "", tags: [] }.
recommendation: Move the at-least-one-of-name-or-tags validation after tag trimming, deduplication, and reserved-tag filtering, and validate against deduplicatedTags.length.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Existing tests cover empty name plus empty tags and cover reserved-tag stripping when a non-empty name remains, but they do not combine tag-only input with only reserved tags.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a PUT route test that sends name: "" and tags: ["arkham", "minipay"] and asserts 400 with no upsertEntry call.
minimumFixScope: ui-dashboard/src/app/api/address-labels/route.ts plus the PUT route test file.

history.note: Fixed by validating non-empty labels after tag trim/dedupe/reserved-tag filtering; added reserved-only tag regression.
